gamma_events.title: Highest temperature in Munich on June 28?
gamma_events.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Munich Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 28 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Munich Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/de/munich/EDDM.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Short-range forecasts from the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts and German Weather Service models currently converge on a maximum of 34–35°C in Munich on June 28 under building high pressure, with partly cloudy skies and possible afternoon thundery showers. This pattern supports the market’s tight clustering around 35–36°C outcomes, as modest ensemble spread arises mainly from uncertainty in convective timing and intensity that could cap peaks through evaporative cooling or allow brief clear intervals to push readings higher. Recent days of above-average warmth across southern Germany have reinforced the setup, while historical June maxima near 30°C provide context for the elevated but not extreme signal. Updated model runs and DWD briefings over the next 48 hours will likely tighten probabilities ahead of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest model consensus from European and global ensembles points to a highest temperature in Munich on June 29 near 27–28 °C, with thundery showers and increased cloud cover expected to limit peak heating. This aligns with the market’s tight clustering around those outcomes, where traders weigh the risk of brief clear spells allowing 29 °C against more persistent instability that caps readings at 27 °C or below. Recent warm advection from the south has raised baselines above the June climatological average of roughly 22–24 °C, but an incoming Atlantic trough introduces timing uncertainty in the exact arrival of precipitation and its cooling effect. Official guidance from agencies such as the German Weather Service (DWD) and ECMWF runs will refine the maximum through the next 48 hours.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the LaGuardia Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 28 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the LaGuardia Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ny/new-york-city/KLGA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models from the National Weather Service and private sources indicate a high in the low 80s °F for New York City on June 28, driven by moderate southerly flow under a building high-pressure ridge with limited moisture and partial cloud cover limiting daytime heating. Recent observations show temperatures near seasonal normals for late June, with no strong heat advection or clear-sky amplification pushing values higher, while light winds and urban heat island effects support the 80–83 °F range that dominates market-implied odds. Ensemble guidance shows tight clustering around these thresholds, with only modest upside risk from any unexpected clearing or downslope warming. Traders appear to weigh this consensus heavily, as the probability of exceeding 85 °F remains low absent a rapid pattern shift before the daily maximum is recorded.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current Japan Meteorological Agency guidance indicates mostly cloudy skies over Tokyo on June 28 amid the early-summer rainy season, limiting solar heating and supporting highs near 24–25 °C. Seasonal outlooks from the same agency project above-normal temperatures through the period due to a strengthened Pacific High, yet short-range model consensus favors modest cooling from increased cloud cover and possible weak frontal passages. These conditions differentiate the closely matched leading outcomes, as even modest shifts in insolation, boundary-layer moisture, or steering flow can alter peak readings by 1–2 °C. Updated model runs and JMA briefings over the next 48 hours will likely drive further market movement.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest model consensus from European and global ensembles points to a highest temperature in Munich on June 29 near 27–28 °C, with thundery showers and increased cloud cover expected to limit peak heating. This aligns with the market’s tight clustering around those outcomes, where traders weigh the risk of brief clear spells allowing 29 °C against more persistent instability that caps readings at 27 °C or below. Recent warm advection from the south has raised baselines above the June climatological average of roughly 22–24 °C, but an incoming Atlantic trough introduces timing uncertainty in the exact arrival of precipitation and its cooling effect. Official guidance from agencies such as the German Weather Service (DWD) and ECMWF runs will refine the maximum through the next 48 hours.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest Met Office guidance points to a 26°C maximum in London on June 28 as the most probable outcome, following the recent extreme heatwave that peaked mid-week with widespread 35–38°C readings. A shift toward cooler Atlantic air and increased cloud cover is expected to moderate temperatures over the weekend, aligning with ensemble model consensus that places the highest likelihood near 25–27°C. Historical June averages hover around 20–22°C, underscoring the lingering warmth, yet forecasters note typical uncertainties from local urban heat islands and small shifts in steering patterns could nudge the peak slightly higher or lower. Upcoming model updates and official briefings this weekend will provide the key data releases influencing final trader positioning.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models from the National Weather Service and private sources indicate a high in the low 80s °F for New York City on June 28, driven by moderate southerly flow under a building high-pressure ridge with limited moisture and partial cloud cover limiting daytime heating. Ensemble guidance shows tight clustering around these thresholds, with only modest upside risk from any unexpected clearing or downslope warming. Traders appear to weigh this consensus heavily, as the probability of exceeding 85 °F remains low absent a rapid pattern shift before the daily maximum is recorded.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/jp/tokyo/RJTT.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current Japan Meteorological Agency guidance indicates mostly cloudy skies over Tokyo on June 28 amid the early-summer rainy season, limiting solar heating and supporting highs near 24–25 °C. Seasonal outlooks from the same agency project above-normal temperatures through the period due to a strengthened Pacific High, yet short-range model consensus favors modest cooling from increased cloud cover and possible weak frontal passages. These conditions differentiate the closely matched leading outcomes, as even modest shifts in insolation, boundary-layer moisture, or steering flow can alter peak readings by 1–2 °C. Updated model runs and JMA briefings over the next 48 hours will likely drive further market movement.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Munich Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/de/munich/EDDM.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest model consensus from European and global ensembles points to a highest temperature in Munich on June 29 near 27–28 °C, with thundery showers and increased cloud cover expected to limit peak heating. This aligns with the market’s tight clustering around those outcomes, where traders weigh the risk of brief clear spells allowing 29 °C against more persistent instability that caps readings at 27 °C or below. Recent warm advection from the south has raised baselines above the June climatological average of roughly 22–24 °C, but an incoming Atlantic trough introduces timing uncertainty in the exact arrival of precipitation and its cooling effect. Official guidance from agencies such as the German Weather Service (DWD) and ECMWF runs will refine the maximum through the next 48 hours.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest Met Office guidance points to a 26°C maximum in London on June 28 as the most probable outcome, following the recent extreme heatwave that peaked mid-week with widespread 35–38°C readings. A shift toward cooler Atlantic air and increased cloud cover is expected to moderate temperatures over the weekend, aligning with ensemble model consensus that places the highest likelihood near 25–27°C. Historical June averages hover around 20–22°C, underscoring the lingering warmth, yet forecasters note typical uncertainties from local urban heat islands and small shifts in steering patterns could nudge the peak slightly higher or lower. Upcoming model updates and official briefings this weekend will provide the key data releases influencing final trader positioning.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the LaGuardia Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ny/new-york-city/KLGA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current Japan Meteorological Agency guidance indicates mostly cloudy skies over Tokyo on June 28 amid the early-summer rainy season, limiting solar heating and supporting highs near 24–25 °C. Seasonal outlooks from the same agency project above-normal temperatures through the period due to a strengthened Pacific High, yet short-range model consensus favors modest cooling from increased cloud cover and possible weak frontal passages. These conditions differentiate the closely matched leading outcomes, as even modest shifts in insolation, boundary-layer moisture, or steering flow can alter peak readings by 1–2 °C. Updated model runs and JMA briefings over the next 48 hours will likely drive further market movement.

Upcoming model updates and official briefings this weekend will provide the key data releases influencing final trader positioning.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/jp/tokyo/RJTT.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current Japan Meteorological Agency guidance indicates mostly cloudy skies over Tokyo on June 28 amid the early-summer rainy season, limiting solar heating and supporting highs near 24–25 °C. Seasonal outlooks from the same agency project above-normal temperatures through the period due to a strengthened Pacific High, yet short-range model consensus favors modest cooling from increased cloud cover and possible weak frontal passages. These conditions differentiate the closely matched leading outcomes, as even modest shifts in insolation, boundary-layer moisture, or steering flow can alter peak readings by 1–2 °C. Updated model runs and JMA briefings over the next 48 hours will likely drive further market movement.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Japan Meteorological Agency forecasts indicate mostly cloudy skies over Tokyo on June 29, with scattered showers possible earlier in the period, which limits daytime heating and supports maximum temperatures near seasonal norms of 25–27°C. Above-average temperature trends for the month, driven by broader atmospheric patterns and long-term warming, keep 26°C and 27°C as the closely matched market leaders, while reduced solar radiation from persistent cloud cover caps upside potential above 28°C. Official guidance shows minimal model divergence for this short-range period, with historical late-June baselines reinforcing the narrow 25–27°C range amid the ongoing rainy season. Traders are monitoring any last forecast updates on cloud breaks or frontal timing ahead of market resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Official National Weather Service forecasts for New York City point to mostly sunny skies and light winds on June 29, supporting surface heating under a building high-pressure ridge with minimal cloud cover or frontal activity. eventMetadata.context_description: Latest model consensus from European and global ensembles points to a highest temperature in Munich on June 29 near 27–28 °C, with thundery showers and increased cloud cover expected to limit peak heating. This aligns with the market’s tight clustering around those outcomes, where traders weigh the risk of brief clear spells allowing 29 °C against more persistent instability that caps readings at 27 °C or below. Recent warm advection from the south has raised baselines above the June climatological average of roughly 22–24 °C, but an incoming Atlantic trough introduces timing uncertainty in the exact arrival of precipitation and its cooling effect. Official guidance from agencies such as the German Weather Service (DWD) and ECMWF runs will refine the maximum through the next 48 hours.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest Met Office guidance points to a 26°C maximum in London on June 28 as the most probable outcome, following the recent extreme heatwave that peaked mid-week with widespread 35–38°C readings. This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the LaGuardia Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 27 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the LaGuardia Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ny/new-york-city/KLGA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest National Weather Service and model guidance indicate a moderating air mass over the Northeast on June 27, with daytime heating tempered by variable cloud cover and a weak frontal boundary. This setup favors maximum temperatures in the upper 70s to low 80s Fahrenheit, aligning with closely matched market probabilities for the 76–77°F, 78–79°F, and 80–81°F bins. Atlantic sea-breeze moderation and early-summer humidity levels typically cap peaks near long-term June climatology for Central Park observations, while minor disagreements among forecast models on cloud timing, wind direction, and boundary-layer mixing sustain the narrow spread traders are pricing. Updated model runs and local NWS briefings through tonight remain the key near-term catalysts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models from the National Weather Service and private sources indicate a high in the low 80s °F for New York City on June 28, driven by moderate southerly flow under a building high-pressure ridge with limited moisture and partial cloud cover limiting daytime heating. Recent observations show temperatures near seasonal normals for late June, with no strong heat advection or clear-sky amplification pushing values higher, while light winds and urban heat island effects support the 80–83 °F range that dominates market-implied odds. Ensemble guidance shows tight clustering around these thresholds, with only modest upside risk from any unexpected clearing or downslope warming. Traders appear to weigh this consensus heavily, as the probability of exceeding 85 °F remains low absent a rapid pattern shift before the daily maximum is recorded.

eventMetadata.context_description: Official National Weather Service forecasts for New York City point to mostly sunny skies and light winds on June 29, supporting surface heating under a building high-pressure ridge with minimal cloud cover or frontal activity. This setup favors a daily maximum in the mid-to-upper 80s, where small differences in afternoon insolation, boundary-layer mixing, or dewpoint advection can determine whether readings peak near 85°F or reach 86–87°F. Ensemble guidance shows tight clustering around these values, reflecting low uncertainty from recent model runs but sensitivity to exact timing of any residual moisture or seabreeze effects along the coast. Historical June climatology for Central Park places average highs near 81°F, so current conditions represent a modest positive anomaly driven by the dominant synoptic pattern.
